route_readiness.project_size: Total eligible grant costs are GBP35,000; project duration 3 months.
route_readiness.lead_requirement: Application must be led and submitted by a UK Knowledge Base partner.
route_readiness.business_requirement: Business partner must be UK registered and have four or more full-time equivalent employees.
route_readiness.contribution_requirement: Business Partner must commit at least 10 percent cash contribution and resource its own costs.
route_readiness.submission_rule: All sections and partner terms must be complete before submission; answers must not include hyperlinks.
route_readiness.why_not_direct_submit: R&A is the business partner candidate, not the eligible Knowledge Base lead, and current evidence does not prove the four-FTE business-partner threshold.

evidence_classes.support: R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd / company number NI696693 can be used for business identity, subject to current Companies House readback.
evidence_classes.grant_use: Business partner identity and title-format support.
evidence_classes.remaining_gate: Does not prove four or more full-time equivalent employees or financial contribution capacity.

evidence_classes.support: Aureon Evidence OS can be framed as a Digital and Technologies knowledge-transfer use case around evidence automation, claim mapping and workflow assurance.
evidence_classes.grant_use: Scope, project summary, innovation need and business challenge narrative.
evidence_classes.remaining_gate: Knowledge Base must agree the work package and validation method.

evidence_classes.support: Prior company/research pack indexed 36 local research outputs and public profile sources.
evidence_classes.grant_use: Capability narrative, knowledge gap, dissemination and validation-plan context.
evidence_classes.remaining_gate: Research outputs are not independent validation and do not replace Knowledge Base leadership.

evidence_classes.support: Existing claim-class controls keep funder language source-grounded and exclude unsupported certainty, legal, finance, defence, energy or validation claims.
evidence_classes.grant_use: Quality-control layer for all AKT6 portal answers and partner-facing materials.
evidence_classes.remaining_gate: Controls do not create missing FTE, budget, cash contribution or lead-applicant evidence.

portal_safe_blocks.text: [Eligible Knowledge Base full legal name] and R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd AKT6
portal_safe_blocks.control: Official AKT6 guidance requires this title pattern and the exact Companies House name for the business.

portal_safe_blocks.text: To evaluate an AI-assisted evidence automation workflow that helps regulated SME teams organise source records, maintain audit trails, classify project claims, and prepare reusable evidence packs for review, compliance and innovation reporting.

R&A Consulting and Brokerage Services Ltd is developing Aureon Evidence OS, a local-first workflow for evidence registers, claim-to-source mapping, reproducible records, risk tagging and review packs. The identified need is that small businesses preparing regulated, technical or grant-funded work often hold evidence across documents, screenshots, ledgers, repositories and partner records, making it hard to prove which claims are supported. The AKT project would pair R&A with an eligible Knowledge Base to evaluate the system in a short, structured validation sprint. The Knowledge Base would help define the method, test the workflow against controlled SME use cases, assess evidence quality and produce a repeatable knowledge-transfer model. The expected opportunity is a clearer evidence-assurance process for SME innovation projects, reducing unsupported claims and improving readiness for funder, partner and customer review.

The project aligns most closely with Digital and Technologies because it evaluates AI-assisted workflow assurance, evidence traceability and controlled reporting for SME operations. The activity is not a pure research project; it is a short knowledge-transfer intervention to evaluate and improve a business innovation with Knowledge Base expertise. The final scope must be confirmed by the Knowledge Base lead and must show the specific knowledge gap, Associate role, work package, deliverables and business benefit.

R&A needs external knowledge-transfer support to convert Aureon Evidence OS from an internal operator workflow into a validated, repeatable SME assurance method. The current system can assemble evidence packs and maintain source-grounded claim controls, but it needs Knowledge Base input on evaluation design, human review, workflow measurement, risk controls and knowledge-transfer documentation. The AKT would accelerate access to academic and innovation-transfer expertise and produce a practical validation sprint for a defined SME use case. It would also clarify whether the model has route-to-market potential as an evidence-assurance service for businesses that must prepare auditable grant, compliance or technical records.

controls: No IFS AKT6 portal draft was opened or mutated.
No email or contact form was sent; EML files are draft-only.
No plaintext passwords, tokens, private keys, bank data, payroll records, raw ledgers, or tax records were copied into this pack.
No unsupported Knowledge Base lead, four-FTE, cash-contribution, fEC/non-fEC, Monitoring Officer invite, terms acceptance, or submission claim was made.
Portal-safe blocks deliberately avoid URLs because the official AKT6 form warns that hyperlinks in answers can cause withdrawal.
